source_title: REGENETEN Advanced Healing Solutions - clinical evidence collection

Percentage of patients with a re-tear assessed on MRI in the control group and REGENETEN Implant groups at 12-months post-operatively 0 10 5 15 25 20 30 Re-tear rate 25.8% 8.3% Percentage of patients (%) p=0.01 Control group REGENETEN group Smith+Nephew ADVANCED HEALING SOLUTIONS Collection of Evidence Isolated bioinductive repair of partial-thickness rotator cuff tears using a resorbable bovine collagen implant: two-year radiologic and clinical outcomes from a prospective multicenter study8 Schlegel TF , Abrams JS, Angelo RL, Getelman MH, Ho CP , Bushnell BD. J Shoulder Elbow Surg. 2021;30:1938–1948. Prospective, multicentre case series evaluating tear size, tendon thickness, pain and function in 33 patients receiving REGENETEN◊ Bioinductive Implant in lieu of standard repair for intermediate- (n=12) or high-grade (n=21) PT tears Overview Results • At 24 months, 87% of tears (26/30) reduced in size by >50% from pre-operative measurements (Figure) • No progression to FT tears in patients compliant with the rehabilitation protocol (n=32) • Improvements in CMS met or exceeded MCIDs in all patients with intermediate-grade tears and 94% of patients with high-grade tears • No significant difference in pain, function or tendon healing were observed in different tear locations, including articular-sided (n=11), bursal-sided (n=10), intrasubstance (n=4) and hybrid (n=8) tears Conclusion The REGENETEN Implant reduced 87% of PT tears in size by >50% at 24 months. Improvements in pain, function, tear healing and tendon thickness were consistent across all tear locations. Figure. Degree of reduction in tear size from baseline to 24 months in patients receiving the REGENETEN Implant †Tear size could not be determined for one patient with a high-grade tear 9.1% 90.9% 15.8% 84.2% >50% reduction in tear size 0–50% reduction in tear size Intermediate-grade tears (n=11) High-grade tears (n=19 †) Percentage of patients 0 20 40 60 80 100 Overall, 87% of tears reduced in size by >50% PT tears Smith+Nephew ADVANCED HEALING SOLUTIONS Evaluation of healing rates and safety with a bioinductive collagen patch for large and massive rotator cuff tears: 2-year safety and clinical outcomes9 Thon SG, O'Malley L 2nd, O'Brien MJ, Savoie FH 3rd. Am J Sports Med. 2019;47:1901–1908. Conclusion In conjunction with double-row repair of large and massive rotator cuff tears, the REGENETEN Implant was safe, induced tissue formation and led to a high tendon healing rate in both primary and revision settings. Prospective study evaluating tendon healing rates, failure rates, tendon thickness, ASES score and safety in 23 patients receiving a REGENETEN◊ Bioinductive Implant in conjunction with double-row repair of large (n=11) or massive (n=12) rotator cuff tears • No implant-related adverse events • Tendon healing in 22/23 patients (96%) on ultrasound at 24 months • Treatment success in 21/23 patients (91%) at 24 months, including 100% (7/7) success in primary repairs and 88% (14/16) in revision repairs (Figure); one additional clinical failure due to progression of glenohumeral osteoarthritis • Mean tendon thickness on ultrasound increased from 6.29mm at 3 months to 7.72mm at 12 months, decreasing to 7.28mm at 24 months Collection of Evidence Next study →Previous studies → Overview Results Full-thickness tearsFT tears Figure. Percentage of patients achieving treatment success in primary and revision repairs Massive tears Treatment success (%) 0 20 40 60 80 100 Overall 100% p=0.33 Large tears 100% 89% p=0.62 100% 86% p=0.38 88% Primary Revision Smith+Nephew ADVANCED HEALING SOLUTIONS Prospective study evaluating re-tear rates, tendon thickness, pain and function in 9 patients with supraspinatus tendon tears (8 medium-sized FT tears, 1 PT tear converted to a FT tear during surgery) who received the REGENETEN◊ Bioinductive Implant in conjunction with standard repair Conclusion The REGENETEN Implant facilitates restoration of the normal tendon footprint and ultimately maintains repair integrity of FT tears at 24 months. Collection of Evidence Next study →Previous studies → Overview Results • No MRI evidence of re-tear or gap formation at 24 months • Significantly greater mean tendon thickness on MRI in study patients versus published values from young, healthy adults with no previous shoulder problems, sustained to 24 months (p<0.01; Figure) • New tissue was indistinguishable from the native tendon on MRI at 12 months • Significant improvements in ASES score and CMS from pre-operative values at 24 months (both p<0.001) Partial-thickness tears Preliminary investigation of a biological augmentation of rotator cuff repairs using a collagen implant: a 2-year MRI follow-up10 Bokor DJ, Sonnabend D, Deady L, et al.
